name: SIGMA - Password Spray Indicator via Failed Logons From a Single Source (Windows Security 4625) [threshold]
description: Elastic threshold companion for rules/sigma/auth_win_bruteforce_source_spray.yml (issue #192, added from a security-auditor review finding: the per-account threshold on 4625 alone misses a password spray, where few passwords are tried against many accounts so no single account's failure count crosses the per-account threshold). Buckets Security 4625 failed logons by source IP (winlog.event_data.IpAddress) and alerts when a single source touches >=6 distinct target accounts (winlog.event_data.TargetUserName cardinality) within a 5-minute window. The Sigma file is the documented logic-of-record; this threshold rule adds the cardinality count that sigma_eval.py / the lucene conversion target cannot express.
author: Suburban-SOC
false_positives: A misconfigured application or service account retrying the same stale credential against many resources from one host.
NAT/proxy gateways where many real users' failed logons share one apparent source IP.
